objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 sport/hobby words, object pronouns (me/him/her/us/them/it), listening, speaking, homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. Спорт ба хоббигийн 20 шинэ үг, объектын төлөөний үг (me/him/her/us/them/it), сонсох, ярих, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Today you learn 20 sports and hobby words and how to use object pronouns me, him, her, us, them, it correctly.
contentMn: Өнөөдөр спорт, хоббигийн 20 үг сурч, me, him, her, us, them, it объектын төлөөний үгсийг зөв ашиглаж сурна.

vocabulary.example: We play volleyball in PE class.
She loves cycling in the park.
He goes to the gym every day.
I am on the school football team.
Don't be sad if you lose.
There is a match on Saturday.

quiz.question: What is the object pronoun for 'he'?
What is the object pronoun for 'she'?
Choose the correct sentence:
quiz.explanation: Object pronoun comes after the verb: he → him.

quiz.question: Give it to _____ (she)
quiz.explanation: After preposition 'to', use object form: her.

quiz.question: What sport does Bat play?
quiz.explanation: From the listening: 'I play football with my friends.'

quiz.question: How do the team feel about the coach?
Which sentence uses an object pronoun correctly?
quiz.explanation: 'Me' is the correct object pronoun after the verb 'help'.

page1.grammarTopic: Object Pronouns — me / him / her / us / them / it
page1.grammarExplanation: Object pronouns REPLACE nouns that receive the action of the verb. They come AFTER the verb or preposition. Subject pronouns (I, you, he, she, we, they) come BEFORE the verb. Object pronouns (me, you, him, her, it, us, them) come AFTER the verb. / Объектын төлөөний үгс нь үйлийг хүлээн авч буй нэрийг орлодог — үйл үгийн ДАРАА ирдэг. Субьектын (I, he, she) үйл үгийн ӨМНӨ, объектын (me, him, her, us, them) ДАРАА ирдэг.
commonMistakes: She likes he. → She likes HIM.
Give it to she. → Give it to HER.
They know we. → They know US.
I love they. → I love THEM.

page2.readingPassage: Bat loves football. He plays it every Saturday. His best friend Dorj also loves football. Bat always passes the ball to him during the game. Their coach is Mr. Gantulga. The team respects him very much. Sarnai loves photography. Her friends ask her to take photos at every event. She takes photos of them and sends them on their phones. Sports and hobbies make us happy!
speakingQuestions: What sport do you like? / Та ямар спортод дуртай вэ?
Do you have a hobby? / Та хоббитой юу?
Who do you play sports with?
Does anyone help you with English?
What sport do you watch on TV?
Can you teach me your hobby? / Та хоббиоо надад заагаад өгнө үү?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I love football! I play it every weekend.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I play with my friends. I always pass the ball to them.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes! Our coach helps us a lot. We respect him.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Can you take a photo of me?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Of course! Smile — I am taking it now!

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Today's topic is sports and hobbies. Bat, what sport do you play? Bat: I play football with my friends. I love it! Teacher: Does anyone help you improve? Bat: Yes! Our coach helps us every Saturday. We all respect him. Teacher: Sarnai, what is your hobby? Sarnai: My hobby is photography. My friends always ask me to take photos of them! Teacher: Do you enjoy it? Sarnai: Yes! I love it. It makes me and my friends very happy.

listeningQuestions.question: How do they feel about the coach?
What do Sarnai's friends ask her?
What does her hobby make them feel?
Which is the object pronoun for 'she'?
Which sentence uses object pronoun correctly?

homework: Writing: Write 5 sentences using object pronouns (me, him, her, us, them).
Vocabulary: Write about your favorite sport or hobby in 3-5 sentences.
Speaking: Ask a friend: 'What sport do you play?' and use 'him/her/them' in your answer.
Review: I→me · he→him · she→her · we→us · they→them · it→it
completionSummary: Used object pronouns naturally in sentences
